title: Cross-file query beyond spec: Brazilian players broken down by Brazilian club
evidence: src/queries.rs:648 brazilian_club_squads() joins FIFA player nationality/club against team keys derived from the match datasets

title: Data-quality hazards handled explicitly with regression tests
evidence: src/store.rs:60 dedups 2012-2019 Brasileirao overlap; src/store.rs:104 ambiguous-base identity layer; tests/sample_questions.rs:95 ambiguous_base regression test, :148 no-double-counting test
suggestion: None — these are correctness safeguards beyond the literal spec

title: 32 tests pass against real datasets (test_coverage=1.0)
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=1.0; tests/sample_questions.rs 27 integration tests + src/normalize.rs 5 unit tests; 0 #[ignore] markers

title: queries.rs concentrates all 12 query functions in one 692-line module
evidence: src/queries.rs is 692 lines; stored maintainability=0.3934 in scores.json reflects module size/complexity
suggestion: Optional: split match-oriented vs player/competition queries into submodules; no functional impact

title: MCP tools return pre-formatted prose strings rather than structured content
evidence: src/server.rs:158 all #[tool] methods return String; e.g. queries::search_matches builds a display string (src/queries.rs:141)
suggestion: Matches TASK.md's example answer format; structured JSON payloads would be more machine-consumable but are not required

title: Player-lookup sample question substitutes Gabriel Jesus for the spec's Gabriel Barbosa (absent from 2019 FIFA snapshot)
evidence: tests/sample_questions.rs:116 who_is_gabriel_jesus() notes Gabigol is not in the dataset and tests Gabriel Jesus instead
suggestion: None — the by-name search capability (R7) is exercised; the substitution is documented in the test
